channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: TikTok creators left in limbo while awaiting decision on potential platform ban
item.description: The fate of TikTok is keeping creators and small business owners in anxious limbo as they await a decision from the Supreme Court that could upheld their livelihoods.
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Landrum Jr. And Haleluya Hadero, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Police took over a week to name the NYC subway burning victim. A fake name spread in the meantime
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — It took police more than a week to publicly identify Debrina Kawam, 57, as the woman who was fatally set on fire in a New York subway train last month. But on the internet, it took just hours for a false name to begin spreading.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: What to know about Apple's $95 million settlement of the snooping Siri case
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit that accused the company of turning its virtual assistant Siri into a snoop that eavesdropped on the users of iPhones and other trendy devices in a betrayal to its long-standing commitment to personal privacy.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Chinese cyber firm linked to botnet attack sanctioned in US
item.description: The federal government hit Chinese cyber group Integrity Technology Group with new sanctions on Friday, citing alleged computer intrusion incidents against those in the U.S. At the direction of the China, hackers associated with the Integrity Technology Group allegedly targeted multiple U.S.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Apple needs good AI acquisition hires
item.description: When it comes to artificial intelligence development, Apple seems more interested than usual in seeking out good acquisition targets, potentially building on the small acquisitions it makes most months. The company has, in fact, led the industry when it comes to strategic AI acquisitions since 2023.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Top 9 generative AI use cases for business
item.description: The launch of ChatGPT in November 2022 set off a generative AI gold rush, with companies scrambling to adopt the technology and demonstrate innovation.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: The cloud architecture renaissance of 2025
item.description: The perfect storm is coming that will force enterprises to rethink their cloud strategy. Cloud architecture will take center stage during 2025. This isn’t just another hype cycle. First, we need to talk about the elephant in the room: generative AI.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Apple to pay $95 million to settle lawsuit accusing Siri of eavesdropping
item.description: Apple has agreed to pay $95 million to settle a lawsuit accusing the privacy-minded company of deploying its virtual assistant Siri to eavesdrop on people using its iPhone and other trendy devices.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: California begins 2025 with solid start to winter snowpack, but more storms are needed
item.description: California is beginning 2025 with a solid start to the winter snowpack, but more storms are needed to keep the state’s water supplies on track.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Health AI: How Apple can boost public health
item.description: Combined with its existing solutions, Apple’s strategic approach to artificial intelligence (AI) deployment could make a radical difference to public health. Here is how it could achieve that.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Nearly a year since launch, Apple’s Vision Pro still searching for a killer app
item.description: Apple launched its highly anticipated Vision Pro “spatial computing” headset last Feb. 2 amid significant hype — and hopes it could finally push virtual or mixed reality into mainstream use.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Agencies required to share custom software under new law
item.description: The SHARE IT Act, now law, builds on previous efforts to ensure agencies are sharing common software code across government. The post Agencies required to share custom software under new law first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Intro to VSCode.dev: The IDE in your browser
item.description: The fully realized browser-based IDE has been a long time coming. Ever since the beginning of the cloud revolution, I’ve thought a powerful and flexible remote IDE was just around the corner. I imagined a development tool that could use any virtual machine as a development platform.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Honey browser extension accused of scamming influencers, users
item.description: In the the 23-minute video by user MegaLag, the PayPal property is accused of abusing the affiliate link system for its own profit.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Rhode Islanders' data was leaked from a cyberattack on state health benefits website
item.description: PROVIDENCE, R.I. (AP) — Cybercriminals who hacked Rhode Island’s system for health and benefits programs have released files to a site on the dark web, a scenario the state has been preparing for, Gov. Daniel McKee said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: The long-term impacts of AI on networking
item.description: AI is either going to spawn a golden age of profits and benefits or kill us all off, according to popular viewpoints on the subject. The latter view has been at least somewhat discredited; if you yell “AI” in a crowded theater these days, hardly anyone runs for the exits.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How to restore deleted text messages on Android
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals ways to restore accidentally deleted text messages on your Android phone.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: These 6 tech questions were settled in 2024
item.description: The Silicon Valley hype cycle follows a familiar pattern: an emerging technology or tech product or service is hinted at, rumored, leaked, reported on, announced, and then shipped. That’s the cycle for what actually happens.
item.dc:creator: Computer World
